name: Parabens
description: Endocrine disruptors linked to hormonal imbalances and reproductive toxicity.

name: Sodium Lauryl Sulfate
description: Harsh detergent that strips natural oils and can cause severe skin and eye irritation.

name: Phthalates
description: Endocrine disruptors associated with developmental and reproductive toxicity.

name: Formaldehyde Releasers
description: Slowly release formaldehyde, a known carcinogen and severe skin sensitizer.

name: Triclosan
description: May contribute to bacterial resistance, endocrine disruption, and thyroid issues.

name: Coal Tar
description: A byproduct of coal processing; a known human carcinogen often found in hair dyes and dandruff shampoos.

name: Ethanolamines
description: Can react to form nitrosamines, which are known carcinogens. Also causes respiratory and skin irritation.

name: Oxybenzone
description: Chemical UV filter linked to hormone disruption and severe coral reef bleaching.

name: PEG Compounds
description: Often contaminated with 1,4-dioxane and ethylene oxide, both known carcinogens.

name: BHA and BHT
description: Synthetic antioxidants used as preservatives; suspected endocrine disruptors and possible carcinogens.

name: Siloxanes
description: Suspected endocrine disruptors and toxic to aquatic wildlife.

name: Hydroquinone
description: Linked to cancer and organ-system toxicity; can cause a skin disease called ochronosis.

name: Methylisothiazolinone
description: Powerful neurotoxin and severe skin allergen/sensitizer.

name: Toluene
description: Toxic solvent that can affect the respiratory system, cause nausea, and has been linked to developmental damage.

name: Carbon Black
description: A dark powder used in eyeliner and mascara; linked to cancer and organ system toxicity.
